evals.prompt: Work on the next task in TASKS.md
evals.expected_output: Reads TASKS.md, picks the first open task, breaks it into TDD-ordered subtasks, updates TASKS.md with subtasks indented under the main task, and creates Task-tool entries for each subtask (work task + commit task pairs), starting with a branch checkout task if a branch is specified.

files.content:
# Project Tasks

- [x] Set up project scaffold
- [ ] Build user authentication module
- [ ] Add REST API for todos

assertions.text: The skill reads TASKS.md and identifies 'Build user authentication module' as the first open task
The subtasks include writing stubs/tests before implementation code (TDD order)
TASKS.md is updated with subtasks indented under the main task
TaskCreate is called at least once to create execution tasks
Each subtask is paired with a commit-and-push task

evals.prompt: Pick up the next item from the checklist and start working
evals.expected_output: Reads TASKS.md, sees a branch instruction, creates a branch checkout task first, then breaks the task into subtasks following TDD, updates TASKS.md, and creates task-tool tasks.

files.content:
# Project Tasks
<!-- branch: feature/csv-parser -->

- [x] Set up repository
- [ ] Implement CSV parser with validation
- [ ] Write CLI interface

assertions.text: The skill detects the branch instruction 'feature/csv-parser' from TASKS.md
A task to checkout or create the 'feature/csv-parser' branch is created before any implementation tasks
Subtasks include writing stubs and failing tests before implementing the CSV parser
TASKS.md is updated with subtasks indented under 'Implement CSV parser with validation'

evals.expected_output: Reads TASKS.md, sees a simple atomic task with no subtasks needed, creates just a work task and a commit task (no complex breakdown required), updates TASKS.md accordingly.

files.content:
# Tasks

- [x] Init repo
- [x] Add .gitignore
- [ ] Rename variable `user_nm` to `username` in src/models/user.py
- [ ] Add logging to all API endpoints

assertions.text: The skill picks 'Rename variable user_nm to username in src/models/user.py' as the first open task (not the logging task)
The skill recognizes this as a simple atomic task and does not overcomplicate it with unnecessary subtasks
A commit & push task is created after the rename task
